title: transport: parse_proxy_addr not tested with missing port
description:
parse_proxy_addr() in core/src/transport.rs is supposed to return Err when the proxy URL has no port (e.g. socks5://127.0.0.1 with no colon after the host). The rsplit_once(':') call would return None, causing the ok_or_else to fire with 'proxy URL missing port'. This error path is not covered by any unit test.

Existing tests cover: socks5h:// IP with port, socks5:// hostname with port, trailing slash stripped, credentials-in-URL (port parses), invalid scheme. The missing-port error case is absent.

Without a test, a refactor of parse_proxy_addr could change the error path and go undetected.
